commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=eef860b364ac89f5a04dd27081dc7b3923bffe53)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Mattermost cache-backed target chat-type inference, propagates `group` through the Mattermost outbound session route, and moves generic outbound `channel:` inference after the plugin hook.

PR surface: Source +52. Total +52 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: a cold Mattermost `channel:<id>` target has no cache hit, core target detection falls back to `group`, and this PR's session route then emits `group`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs author repair plus contributor-provided real Mattermost proof, so it should stay in human review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(mattermost): implement inferTargetChatType hook to resolve private channel chat_type
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Mattermost private channels (type `P`) are authoritatively typed as `group` by `mapMattermostChannelTypeToChatType`, but are addressed as `channel:<id>` — the same prefix as public channels. The generic `inferChatTypeFromTarget` in `targets.ts` matched the `channel:` prefix and returned `"channel"` before the plugin hook could override it. This caused the same conversation to be keyed under two different session namespaces (`group:<id>` inbound vs `channel:<id>` delivery), splitting thread delivery across incompatible session keys.

## Why This Change Was Made

Two fixes work together:

1. **`src/infra/outbound/targets.ts`**: Reordered `inferChatTypeFromTarget` to consult the plugin `inferTargetChatType` hook BEFORE the `channel:`/`thread:` prefix default. Previously the prefix match short-circuited and the hook was never reached for `channel:` targets. Now plugins can override the chat type for ambiguous prefixes. The unambiguous `user:` and `group:` prefixes are still checked first.

2. **`extensions/mattermost/`**: Implemented Mattermost missing `inferTargetChatType` hook (Telegram, Discord, Signal, iMessage, Slack, ClickClack, WhatsApp all already implement it). The hook resolves `user:`→direct, `group:`→group, and `channel:<id>` via a module-level cache populated by the monitor when channel info is fetched through the existing `resolveChannelInfo` path. Also fixed `resolveMattermostOutboundSessionRoute` to propagate `"group"` chatType when `resolvedTarget.kind === "group"`.

## Changes

- **`src/infra/outbound/targets.ts`** — reorder `inferChatTypeFromTarget`: plugin hook before `channel:`/`thread:` default
- **`extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts`** — add `inferTargetChatType` hook consulting cache
- **`extensions/mattermost/src/session-route.ts`** — propagate `group` chatType from resolvedTarget
- **`extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-gating.ts`** — add module-level `mattermostChannelKindCache` + `setMattermostChannelKindCache`
- **`extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-resources.ts`** — populate channel kind cache in `resolveChannelInfo`
- **`extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts`** — add import for future cache use

## Evidence

### Design verification

The `inferTargetChatType` hook pattern is already implemented by 7 other channel plugins. Tested approaches in existing plugins:

- **Telegram** (`channel.ts:812`): `inferTargetChatType: ({ to }) => resolveTelegramRouteTarget(to).chatType` — carries chatType on route target
- **Discord** (`channel.ts:338`): `inferTargetChatType: ({ to }) => parseDiscordTarget(to, { defaultKind: "channel" })?.kind`
- **Signal** (`channel.ts:304`): `inferTargetChatType: ({ to }) => inferSignalTargetChatType(to)`

The Mattermost implementation follows the same established pattern, with a module-level cache for the channel-type resolution that the prefix-based target string cannot encode.

### Existing test coverage

The Mattermost plugin has existing tests for `mapMattermostChannelTypeToChatType` in:
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-gating.test.ts`
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.channel-kind.test.ts`

The targets module has existing tests exercising `inferTargetChatType` in:
- `src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts`

## Related

Closes #95646

commands.author: xydt-tanshanshan

target.title: fix(agents): add tool-activity heartbeat to keep subagent alive during tool calls
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Subagent sessions are killed by the 120s LLM idle timeout while performing long-running tool calls, even though the subagent is actively collecting data and within its total `runTimeoutSeconds` budget. The LLM idle watchdog resets only on token arrival — tool completions do not signal it.

## Summary

- **Problem**: The LLM idle-timeout watchdog (`streamWithIdleTimeout`) only resets on LLM token arrival. During subagent tool calls (web_fetch, read, exec, etc.), the LLM stream is idle, so a subagent performing multi-minute data collection is killed at 120s despite being well within its 900s total run budget.
- **Solution**: Implement a per-run tool-activity heartbeat:
  1. `notifyToolActivity(runId)` — published from embedded-runner tool wrappers whenever a tool completes (success + error)
  2. `onToolActivity(runId, listener)` — consumed by `streamWithIdleTimeout` to reset idle watchdog on tool completion
  3. `getLastToolActivityMs(runId)` — shared timestamp Map, visible to `armTimer` before first stream iteration
  4. `clearToolActivityRun(runId)` — wired into run lifecycle `finally` block (success, error, abort, cancel, restart)
- **What changed**: 6 files (+238/-7)
    - `src/shared/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` (new) — per-run Maps for listeners + timestamps + cleanup
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` (new) — barrel re-export
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts` (new) — 7 tests (pub/sub, per-run scoping, cleanup)
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts` — accept `runId`, use `getLastToolActivityMs`, heartbeat subscription, stale timestamp guard
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts` — 2 heartbeat integration tests
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — wrap `effectiveTools` execute with `notifyToolActivity`, `clearToolActivityRun` in `finally`
- **What did NOT change**: No config changes, no provider/channel/UI changes. Idle timeout default unchanged.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes: [#94124](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94124)

## Motivation

When a subagent performs tool calls that take minutes, the LLM stream is idle. The 120s idle timeout kills the session even though the subagent is actively gathering data. The per-run scoping prevents concurrent runs from resetting each other's idle watchdogs, and the lifecycle cleanup prevents memory leaks in long-running gateways.

ClawSweeper-identified defects addressed across iterations:
1. **Unused `unsubscribeToolActivity`** (lint) — removed binding
2. **Process-global heartbeat scope** — replaced with per-run `Map<runId>` scoping
3. **Missing lifecycle cleanup** — `clearToolActivityRun` in `finally` block
4. **Stale timestamp 1ms watchdog** — `recentActivity` guard filters timestamps older than `timeoutMs`

## Evidence

### Unit tests (85/85 passing)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts --run
 Tests  7 passed (7)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts --run
 Tests  78 passed (78)
```

### Live subagent run

- **Environment**: Linux x86_64, Node 22.19+, OpenClaw 2026.6.9, branch `fix/subagent-tool-heartbeat-94124`
- **Command**:

```bash
pnpm openclaw agent --agent main \
  --message "Use subagent to spawn a child task. The child agent should use write_file to write a timestamp and 'Hello from subagent proof test' to /tmp/subagent-proof.txt"
```

- **Output**:

```
Task completed successfully. /tmp/subagent-proof.txt written.
[agent] run 242d29b1-... ended with stopReason=stop
```

```console
$ cat /tmp/subagent-proof.txt
Hello from subagent proof at 2026-06-22 08:55:00 CST (+0800)
```

### Evidence matrix

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| Subagent dispatched and ran | ✅ |
| Tool (`write_file`) executed by subagent | ✅ |
| Output file created with correct content | ✅ |
| `LLM idle timeout` error in output | ❌ Not present |
| `stopReason` | `stop` (normal completion) |
| `clearToolActivityRun` in `finally` block | ✅ `attempt.ts:5773` |
| Stale timestamp > timeout → ignored | ✅ `recentActivity` guard |
| Concurrent run isolation | ✅ `Map<runId>` scoping |

- **What was not tested**: Multi-minute long-running tool call with `web_fetch` or `exec` — the `write_file` tool path exercises the same heartbeat code path. A >120s exec call would require a model that reliably dispatches exec within subagents.

## Root Cause

The LLM idle timeout watchdog in `streamWithIdleTimeout` observed only LLM request activity. No tool-completion path reset the watchdog, so subagents performing tool work were killed at 120s.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Subagents executing long-running tool calls will no longer be killed mid-execution with "LLM idle timeout (120s): no response from model".

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: Unit test pub/sub correctness, per-run scoping, cleanup removal, timer reset on tool activity, cross-turn persistence, stale timestamp filtering
- **Edge cases checked**: No prior tool activity (full timeout), tool activity before stream creation (effective timeout), multiple resets within same stream, stale timestamp from previous stream
- **What you did NOT verify**: Multi-minute long-running tool call with `web_fetch` or `exec`

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — no API or config changes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. Per-run Maps for listener/timestamp state, dual-level subscription (wrapper for cross-turn persistence, per-stream for immediate reset). All tool paths covered at `effectiveTools` assembly. Lifecycle cleanup via `finally` block.
- **Refactor needed**: No.
- **Alternative considered**: PR #94133 uses signal-keyed WeakMap with mismatched signals, only covers cataloged tools, lacks cross-turn persistence and lifecycle cleanup.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: The heartbeat polling in `attempt.ts` wraps every tool execute function. Overhead is a synchronous function call per tool invocation — negligible.
- **Mitigation**: `clearToolActivityRun(params.runId)` in the run lifecycle `finally` block ensures Maps do not retain entries for completed runs.
